title: Onobroma carlinoides
taxonomy: Onobroma carlinoides is a species in the family Ochnaceae. This family is part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is part of the clade rosids, which is a subgroup of flowering plants. The clade rosids is part of the larger clade of flowering plants called angiosperms. intro: Onobroma carlinoides, also known as Cariniana legalis, is a plant species belonging to the family Lecythidaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Onobroma carlinoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Amazonian herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being made into a cough syrup to soothe respiratory problems.
